questions.q: Which monkey hero set the city of Lanka ablaze in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the vanara messenger of Rama, burned Lanka after the rakshasas set his tail on fire.

questions.q: What part of Hanuman's body did the rakshasas set on fire?
questions.explanation: The rakshasas wrapped cloth around Hanuman's tail and set it alight as a punishment.

questions.q: What was Lanka famously made of, giving it the epithet of the 'golden city'?
questions.explanation: Lanka was renowned as a golden city, its palaces gleaming with wealth, which Hanuman set ablaze.

questions.q: Who was the demon king ruling Lanka at the time of its burning?
questions.explanation: Ravana was the ten-headed rakshasa king of Lanka, holding Sita captive there.

questions.q: Why had Hanuman traveled to Lanka before it was burned?
questions.explanation: Hanuman crossed the ocean to Lanka on a mission to find the captive Sita and bring word to Rama.

questions.q: Whom was Hanuman serving when he carried out his mission to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was the devoted servant and messenger of Rama, prince of Ayodhya.

questions.q: How did Hanuman spread the fire across Lanka after his tail was lit?
questions.explanation: With his tail aflame, Hanuman bounded from rooftop to rooftop, setting the mansions of Lanka on fire.

questions.q: In which body of water is the island of Lanka situated?
questions.explanation: Lanka was an island city across the ocean from the mainland, which Hanuman leapt over to reach it.

questions.q: What kind of beings inhabited Lanka and captured Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Lanka was a city of rakshasas, the demon race ruled by Ravana, who captured Hanuman.

questions.q: After burning Lanka, where did Hanuman go?
options: He bounded back home across the sea
questions.explanation: Having set the city ablaze, Hanuman leapt back across the ocean to rejoin Rama's allies.

questions.q: Which section (kanda) of Valmiki's Ramayana narrates the burning of Lanka?
questions.explanation: The Sundara Kanda recounts Hanuman's journey to Lanka, his meeting with Sita, and the burning of the city.

questions.q: Whose son captured Hanuman using the Brahmastra after their fight in the Ashoka grove?
questions.explanation: Indrajit (Meghanada), son of Ravana, bound Hanuman with the Brahmastra and brought him to Ravana's court.

questions.q: In which garden was Sita held captive, where Hanuman found her before the burning?
questions.explanation: Sita was kept in the Ashoka Vatika, the grove where Hanuman located her and delivered Rama's ring.

questions.q: What punishment did Ravana originally order for Hanuman before his ministers intervened?
questions.explanation: Ravana ordered Hanuman killed, but Vibhishana argued a messenger should not be slain, so the tail-burning was chosen instead.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's brothers argued that an envoy like Hanuman should not be killed?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, the righteous brother of Ravana, insisted that killing a messenger violated dharma.

questions.q: What did the rakshasas wrap around Hanuman's tail to fuel the fire?
questions.explanation: They bound oil-soaked rags and cloth around his tail before setting it alight.

questions.q: According to tradition, why did the fire not burn or harm Hanuman himself?
options: Sita's prayer made fire cool toward him
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that by Sita's prayer the fire god made the flames cool and harmless to Hanuman's body even as his tail blazed.

questions.q: Before being captured, Hanuman caused destruction in Ravana's pleasure garden. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Hanuman uprooted trees and wrecked the Ashoka grove to provoke the rakshasas and assess Lanka's strength.

questions.q: What token did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama as proof she lived?
questions.explanation: Sita entrusted Hanuman with her Chudamani, the hair-jewel, as a sign to Rama that she still lived.

questions.q: What ability did Hanuman use to change his size, paraded through Lanka with a burning tail?
options: Altering his body's size at will
questions.explanation: Hanuman could expand or shrink his form at will, a power he used throughout his exploits in Lanka.

questions.q: How was Hanuman led through the streets of Lanka before he broke free and burned it?
options: Paraded as a captive with his tail aflame
questions.explanation: The rakshasas paraded the bound Hanuman through Lanka's streets with his tail burning, intending to humiliate him.

questions.q: Whose dwelling did Hanuman deliberately spare from the flames during the burning of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman spared the house of the righteous Vibhishana, who had defended him in Ravana's court.

questions.q: What sudden worry struck Hanuman after Lanka was ablaze, concerning his mission?
options: That he had lost Sita's jewel
That Sita might have perished in the fire
That Rama would be angry at the delay
That the ocean had dried up
questions.explanation: After the burning, Hanuman feared the flames might have harmed Sita, until celestial voices reassured him she was unharmed.

questions.q: Which female rakshasi guarding Lanka did Hanuman defeat upon first entering the city by night?
questions.explanation: Hanuman struck down Lankini, the guardian spirit of the city, whose defeat was an omen of Lanka's doom.

questions.q: Which mighty son of Ravana did Hanuman kill in battle before being finally captured?
questions.explanation: Hanuman slew Aksha, a young son of Ravana sent against him, before Indrajit subdued him with the Brahmastra.

questions.q: Why did Hanuman allow himself to remain bound even after the Brahmastra's hold had naturally lapsed?
options: He was too weak to escape
Out of respect, to honor Brahma's weapon and to meet Ravana
He was chained with iron as well
questions.explanation: Hanuman submitted to the bonds out of reverence for the Brahmastra and a wish to confront Ravana directly, though he could have freed himself.

questions.q: After Hanuman burned Lanka and feared for Sita, what reassured him she was safe?
options: The words of celestial beings / charanas
questions.explanation: Celestial voices (charanas) declared that Sita was unharmed, calming Hanuman's fears after the fire.

questions.q: How did Hanuman extinguish his still-burning tail before leaving Lanka, according to tradition?
options: He dipped it in the ocean's waters
questions.explanation: Tradition relates that Hanuman quenched his flaming tail in the sea before bounding back across the ocean.

questions.q: What strategic purpose, beyond revenge, did Hanuman's burning of Lanka serve for Rama's cause?
options: It demonstrated vanara strength and shook Lanka's morale
It built a bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: The burning showed the rakshasas the power of even a single vanara, sowing fear before Rama's army arrived.

questions.q: Upon returning from Lanka, with what cry did Hanuman announce his success to the waiting vanaras?
questions.explanation: Hanuman proclaimed 'Drishta Sita' — 'I have seen Sita' — joyfully announcing that he had found her alive.
